query: I keep re-explaining how our team does API error responses. Make a skill for it.
expected_behavior: Chooses the 'knowledge' archetype (reference applied inline, not a /command)
Writes >= 3 eval cases before writing the skill body
Produces a third-person description containing an explicit 'Use when' trigger
Keeps the body an overview and pushes detail to references one level deep
Runs validate_skill.py and reports it clean before declaring done
